constraints: Allow several distinct factions in the eventual campaign
Use exactly one cohesive opposing faction in the first complete level
Give the MVP faction a readable visual language focused roster and faction-appropriate climax
Do not build assets or systems for future factions before the first level is complete and enjoyable
Use later factions to expand enemy silhouettes movement pressures environments bosses and narrative conflict
Keep first-level loot and presentation coherent with the chosen faction and location

open_questions_added: Specific enemy faction identity for the one-level MVP
First level location mission objective and climax
Minimum enemy roster and boss boundary for the vertical slice
Art-production pipeline for dimensional 2D faction assets

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether the one-level internal MVP fights an automated industrial or rogue-machine faction, a human corporate-security or mercenary faction, or a biological or alien infestation faction. Preserve the broader multi-faction future setting while keeping the first level entirely focused on one production-ready faction. Include visual readability, animation and asset cost, fit with four-weapon mech combat and loot, enemy-role variety, boss potential, environmental cohesion, story flexibility, and reuse into later missions. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Continue queuing D-142 through D-150 and persist the complete remaining batch at D-150 unless another context handoff or explicit user instruction requires earlier persistence.
